data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c851c3dd">
    <title>agent: add default adversarial pre-close review gate</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ADR-024 and FEAT-010 now require adversarial review to be the default pre-close gate, but current loop/review code still models single post-merge review and reopen behavior. A successful implementation can be closed even though the review path is not modeled as a two-reviewer unanimous pre-close gate with repair-cycle traceability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 documents ReviewVerdict as post-merge review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 documents Reopen as the REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK path after a bead was closed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:474 builds one reviewer request, without review_group_id or reviewer_index correlation metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:528 runs a single DefaultBeadReviewer.ReviewBead invocation and parses one verdict.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:242 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:279 assert REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK reopen behavior instead of pre-close blocking behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a pre-close review coordinator used by execute-loop after a close-eligible implementation result and before CloseWithEvidence.
- Default behavior runs two no-tool reviewer invocations against the same bounded evidence bundle. Each invocation carries role=reviewer plus bead_id, attempt_id, result_rev, review_group_id, reviewer_index, and implementer route facts.
- Close only when both reviewers return APPROVE with per-AC evidence. Treat APPROVE without required evidence as review_error: unparseable.
- Any evidenced REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK prevents close and classifies the result into review_fixable_gap, review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, or review_error.
- review_fixable_gap schedules a bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. Terminal classifications block the bead with needs_human or decomposition metadata as specified by TD-031.
- Review transport/provider/parse errors retry per result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries; exhaustion emits review-manual-required and blocks without closing.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL review-session UI mutations.
- Manual human conversation review sessions.
- Changing the evidence assembler beyond adding the review-group fields required by this gate.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Execute-loop invokes the adversarial pre-close review coordinator before CloseWithEvidence for every close-eligible implementation result unless an explicit audited override disables review.
2. TestPreCloseReview_DefaultRunsTwoNoToolReviewers verifies two reviewer invocations receive the same evidence bundle and correlation fields role=reviewer, review_group_id, reviewer_index=0/1, bead_id, attempt_id, result_rev, and implementer route facts.
3. TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses verifies close occurs only when both reviewers APPROVE and each approval includes per-AC evidence.
4. TestPreCloseReview_ApproveWithoutEvidenceIsReviewError verifies an evidence-free APPROVE is classified as review_error: unparseable and does not close the bead.
5. TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesBlocksCloseAndSchedulesRepair verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK with fixable implementation findings records review-fixable-gap, leaves the bead unclosed, and starts the next implementation cycle with repair_context_from_review_group.
6. TestPreCloseReview_TerminalClassificationsBlockOperator verifies review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error block with needs_human and do not schedule unbounded retries.
7. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewErrorRetryBudgetIsPerReviewerSlot verifies review errors retry by result_rev + reviewer_index up to review_max_retries, then emit review-manual-required and block without close.
8. TestPreCloseReview_NoClosedBeadReopen verifies REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK never closes and reopens a bead; the failed review prevents close in the first place.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:18, area:agent, kind:feature, reliability, adr:024</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-42b917fe" base-rev="4e2d0190f827b9cf20543491c612bf1cc2df66b9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output_summary: out=7.0KB 186 lines "} else if attemptOut.Disposition == agenttry...."

data.output:
I did not implement the bead.

Reason: the requested change is too large for this execution bead as scoped, and this bead is already at decomposition depth 2, so I cannot split it further here. I recorded the blocker in [.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c851c3dd-20260506T025136-f08b536c/.ddx/executions/20260506T025136-f08b536c/no_changes_rationale.txt).
